grades.expectation: Response identifies this as requiring enhanced due diligence (EDD)
grades.reason: Clearly identifies EDD requirement under Regulation 33 of MLR 2017 and explains why Dubai triggers this

grades.expectation: Response mentions source country risk assessment or FATF status
grades.reason: Explicitly mentions UAE's removal from FATF Grey List in February 2024 and explains why many firms still consider it high risk

grades.expectation: Response requires SWIFT/wire transfer confirmations or equivalent transfer evidence
grades.reason: Specifically mentions MT103 document requirement and includes it in the summary checklist
Addresses currency exchange houses and requires verification they are UAE Central Bank regulated

grades.expectation: Response mentions suspicious activity report (SAR) obligation if concerns arise
grades.reason: Explicitly mentions SAR filing obligation to NCA when client is evasive and discusses Required Terms
